mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent within a single quarter, labor market tightening accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day returns do not differ significantly between joint-up and joint-down periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a subsequent rebound in INDPRO signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive after two consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain negative or flat in over 60% of INDPRO rebound episodes, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during supply contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reduces recession probabilities and stabilizes corporate earnings forecasts, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.05 for three consecutive years, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.50 percent, the Federal Reserve signals a tightening bias that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a two-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a simple momentum baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts aggregate consumption demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month period.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises in the same month
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day returns do not significantly outperform during months where both series move favorably.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, a sustained drop in retail sales signals demand destruction and stagflation risk, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as margin compression accelerates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3.0% YoY and RSAFS falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during periods where CPI stays below 2.5% despite falling retail sales.
